You can navigate by clicking on names or status counts to filter specific results.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Within the ","marks":[],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"Log view","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":", you’ll find detailed logs and attachments for each test.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"As you review your test results and inspect logs, you’ll likely notice some failed or unstable cases. That’s where ","marks":[],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"hyperlink","data":{"uri":"https://reportportal.io/blog/defect-triage-with-reportportal"},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"defect triage","marks":[{"type":"underline"},{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"text","value":" comes in — the next step of the process, where you analyze the root cause of each failure and classify it properly. For any failed item, you can assign an appropriate defect type:","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"To Investigate","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":" – not yet reviewed ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Product Bug","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":" – issue in the application ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Automation Bug","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":" – problem in the test or data ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"System Issue","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":" – environment failure ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"No Defect","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":" – false alarm or expected outcome","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"heading-2","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"4. Visualize Test Data","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"After reviewing and triaging your test results, it’s time to turn data into insights.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"ReportPortal provides rich ","marks":[],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"hyperlink","data":{"uri":"https://reportportal.io/blog/test-results-visualization"},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"visualizations","marks":[{"type":"underline"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"text","value":" to help you track testing trends and quality metrics in real time. Dashboards can include various widgets — such as ","marks":[],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"hyperlink","data":{"uri":"https://reportportal.io/docs/dashboards-and-widgets/OverallStatistics"},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Overall statistics","marks":[{"type":"underline"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"text","value":", ","marks":[],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"hyperlink","data":{"uri":"https://reportportal.io/docs/dashboards-and-widgets/ComponentHealthCheck"},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Component health check","marks":[{"type":"underline"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"text","value":", ","marks":[],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"hyperlink","data":{"uri":"https://reportportal.io/docs/dashboards-and-widgets/PassingRateSummary"},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Passing rate summary","marks":[{"type":"underline"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"text","value":", ","marks":[],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"hyperlink","data":{"uri":"https://reportportal.io/docs/dashboards-and-widgets/FlakyTestCasesTableTop50"},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Flaky test cases table","marks":[{"type":"underline"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"text","value":" — giving you instant visibility into your automation performance.  To quickly build a dashboard tailored to your own test data:","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"ordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Generate demo data","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"

","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"embedded-asset-block","data":{"target":{"sys":{"id":"XaQUoBu3TdLYg4zWLVD5Y","type":"Link","linkType":"Asset"}}},"content":[]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Once you save the changes, the demo dashboard will automatically refresh, and all widgets will display metrics based on your actual test runs.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"embedded-asset-block","data":{"target":{"sys":{"id":"12RasBa925uxMbdGYLhSOA","type":"Link","linkType":"Asset"}}},"content":[]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"This quick start guide has shown how easily you can begin working with ReportPortal — from exploring the demo environment to deploying your own instance, integrating it with your test framework, reviewing results, and visualizing ","marks":[],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"hyperlink","data":{"uri":"https://reportportal.io/docs/dashboards-and-widgets/ReportingAndMetricsInReportPortal#qa-and-test-automation-metrics-in-reportportal"},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"key metrics","marks":[{"type":"underline"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"text","value":". With just a few steps, you can turn raw test execution data into clear, actionable information about product quality and test stability. Whether you’re running small regression suites or managing large-scale CI pipelines, ReportPortal helps you centralize results, speed up failure analysis, and make data-driven decisions for continuous improvement in testing.","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}

leadParagraph.leadParagraph: ReportPortal is an open-source test automation dashboard designed to collect, analyze, and visualize results from your automated test runs in real time. It helps QA and DevOps teams focus on what really matters — identifying failures, tracking defects, and improving test stability over time.

title.title: Benefits of ReportPortal for the AI and Software Sector

leadParagraph.leadParagraph: Software testing is becoming more complex, especially with the rise of AI-driven products and large-scale systems. Teams need tools that don’t just collect test results but help them understand and act on the data.

{"nodeType":"document","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"TL;DR for busy leaders","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Avoid using real production data in tests and never include sensitive data in automated test reports. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"ReportPortal addresses confidentiality, integrity, availability, and compliance of reported data. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Security in ReportPortal spans the full lifecycle: secure development, encryption, IAM, retention, auditing, and monitoring.","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Test automation reporting tools collect rich contextual artifacts — logs, stack traces, screenshots, and attachments — that may unintentionally include personally identifiable information (PII), secrets, or other sensitive business data. This article outlines potential threat scenarios and describes how ReportPortal helps reduce these risks.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"heading-2","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Why Test Reporting Needs Security & Privacy","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"When you run automated tests, they generate a lot of helpful data for debugging. The problem is some of the data may accidentally reveal information meant to stay private. Sometimes teams slip up and use real information in tests — it happens. That’s why ReportPortal is designed to keep your data safe, even if something sensitive ends up in the reports. And since regulations like GDPR and CCPA plus audits for SOC 2 or ISO 27001 require strict data handling, it’s important to treat test results with the same care as production logs.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Types of sensitive data that commonly sneak into reports:","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Logs & stack traces","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"
Error messages or SQL queries might expose user IDs, emails, or tokens. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Screenshots","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"
A simple UI snapshot could show personal details, payment info, or internal URLs. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Environment variables","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"
Automated tests often rely on environment variables for configuration, such as API keys, database URLs, or feature flags. If these variables are logged, captured in reports, or included in attachments, they can expose sensitive information unintentionally. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Secrets in test code","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"
Hard-coded credentials or temporary keys left behind for convenience. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Typical risk scenarios:","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Screenshot leak","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"
A test screenshot catches a customer email or card number and then gets stored in a shared project. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Long-lived artifacts","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"
Some logs or attachments stick around forever, carrying sensitive details. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Excessive permissions","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"
Someone gets assigned the wrong role — for example, a Project Manager instead of a Member — and can accidentally modify important settings. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Security isn’t something you enable once — it’s a continuous process that runs through every layer of the product and every phase of its lifecycle. From how features are developed to how data is stored, accessed, and monitored, ReportPortal applies security best practices end to end.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"heading-2","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Secure Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC)","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Even when sensitive data is reported, ReportPortal ensures data safety and security. This is achieved through a process that covers every stage — from design and coding to testing, deployment, and operations:","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Secure architecture","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"
Threat modeling, security risk assessments and security impact analyses are conducted to identify where sensitive data flows and what controls are needed. Detailed architecture documents and diagrams can be shared with customers under NDA. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Secure development","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"
Developers receive training on secure coding, PII/GDPR awareness and how to avoid anti-patterns that leak secrets. The codebase is scanned regularly with static analysis tools to catch common issues (unsafe deserialization, hard-coded secrets, insecure logging). ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Secure testing","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"
Application security is tested continuously: static analysis + dynamic scans, and bi-annual penetration tests to validate controls against current threats. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Secure deployment","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"
Encryption in transit and at rest is enforced. Deployments follow recommended best practices from cloud providers and include infrastructure hardening — such as secure network segmentation, strict access policies, regular patching, and vulnerability management. Encryption of data in transit is done via TLS for UI/API. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Secure operations","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"
Segregation of duties and least-privilege access are enforced operationally. Infrastructure monitoring and alerting (SIEM, metrics dashboards such as Grafana) surface anomalous behavior. Data retention and lifecycle policies limit exposure from stale artifacts. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Annual security audit","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"
Once a year, ReportPortal undergoes a comprehensive audit covering secure development practices, testing procedures, and deployment processes. This review ensures that security controls remain effective, up to date with evolving threats, and aligned with compliance requirements.","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"embedded-asset-block","data":{"target":{"sys":{"id":"6F6hSEOMEyeaYKf6TJgt2P","type":"Link","linkType":"Asset"}}},"content":[]},{"nodeType":"heading-2","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Identity & Access Management (IAM)","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Proper identity and access controls are the first line of defense.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Authentication","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"
ReportPortal supports enterprise SSO integrations (SAML, LDAP, Active Directory). ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Authorization","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"
Data is organized by projects. Role-based access control lets you assign roles such as Project Manager, Operator, Customer, Member — each role has scoped permissions (view, edit). Apply least privilege: give users the minimum role needed. ReportPortal also offers ","marks":[],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"hyperlink","data":{"uri":"https://reportportal.io/docs/features/SCIMServerFeature"},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"SCIM Server","marks":[{"type":"underline"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"text","value":" feature, which automates user provisioning and project assignments. This reduces manual work and minimizes the risk of errors during account setup and role management. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"API access","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"
Users create personal access tokens for automation and CI. Tokens are revocable and you should enforce token rotation policies. Instance-level timeouts are configurable, so inactive sessions automatically expire. When combined with SSO and conditional access policies, session controls reduce the risk from stolen or unattended sessions.","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"heading-2","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Retention & Deletion","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Controlling how long artifacts live is essential to limiting risk.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Configurable retention","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"
Launches, logs, screenshots and attachments have configurable retention period. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Automated cleanup","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"
Scheduled cleanup jobs remove artifacts past their retention window. This prevents accumulation of long-lived secrets. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Inactive user cleanup & right to be forgotten","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"
Automated policies can remove inactive users after a configurable period. ReportPortal supports “right to be forgotten” flows: an instance variable can enable account deletion, and deleting a user removes personal data associated with that account. These trails are essential for forensic investigations and for proving compliance during audits.","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"heading-2","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Governance & Compliance","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"ReportPortal is open-source solution, giving you full flexibility without vendor lock-in. ReportPortal handles infrastructure and operations. The following examples highlight frequent issues and practical ways to address them:","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Secrets in stack traces","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"
Add test-runner filters and regex scrubbing to redact credentials before report submission. Implement pre-publish hooks that scan artifacts for secrets. Check out the guide for practical steps on ","marks":[],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"hyperlink","data":{"uri":"https://reportportal.io/blog/exclude-sensitive-information-from-logs-in-reportportal"},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"excluding sensitive information from logs in ReportPortal","marks":[{"type":"underline"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"text","value":".","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Overshared projects","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"
Apply least-privilege roles within the project and regularly review permissions. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Never-expiring artifacts","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"
Enforce retention and lifecycle policies. Avoid marking many launches as “important” unless necessary. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"“God-mode” API tokens","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"

Configure your network properly, including reverse proxies if needed, and provision certificates to enforce TLS for UI and API traffic. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"At-rest encryption","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"
Enable encryption for all stored data, including logs and attachments, and implement key rotation policies to maintain security over time. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Identity management","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"
Integrate SSO using SAML, LDAP or Active Directory. Local accounts should be used only as a break-glass accounts. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Roles and permissions","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"
Define roles per project and create least-privilege templates to ensure users have only the access necessary for their tasks. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Retention policies","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"
Set configurable retention rules for launches, logs, and attachments to limit exposure of sensitive data. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Audit logging and alerting","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"
Use audit log dashboards and configure alerts to monitor critical actions, such as role changes, or unusual activity.","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Test reporting is a critical part of the software development process, but it comes with unique security and privacy challenges. ReportPortal provides a comprehensive set of controls — from development practices and encryption to identity management, retention policies, and auditing — to help teams protect sensitive data throughout its lifecycle. By following best practices, configuring your instance carefully, and regularly reviewing access and retention policies, you can minimize risks, maintain compliance, and ensure that test artifacts remain both useful and protected. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}

title.title: Privacy First: How ReportPortal Keeps Test Data Under Control

leadParagraph.leadParagraph: Test automation reporting tools collect rich contextual artifacts — logs, stack traces, screenshots, and attachments — that may unintentionally include personally identifiable information (PII), secrets, or other sensitive business data.
